stages.name: targeted_unit_tests
stages.evidence: doctest_mutate_stabilization_test covers: the stabilized pass rejects non-opt-in documentation (error.NotOptedIn); deterministic byte-exact snapshots for a killed and a survived documentation mutant; a survived mutant gets a durable ds_ survivor ref and a dm_ case id while killed/skipped mutants get none; runner_evidence carries failure_kind on every mutation case; identical inputs reproduce identical dm_/ds_ ids (repeatability); a normal doctest failure blocks mutation-aware execution (preflight -> single skipped entry, no mutants); the doctest.report.v1 schema defines the additive case.mutation, summary.mutation, mutation_runner_evidence (with required failure_kind) and the widened status enum; and dm_/ds_ derivation is deterministic and distinct across namespaces; full suite green

stages.name: determinism_tests
stages.evidence: dm_ and ds_ ids are sha256 + lowercase Crockford base32 over the documented canonical field order (no display order, wall-clock, absolute paths, command output, or status); the stable report is sorted by dm_ id; survivor refs are emitted only for survived mutants; the mutation extension is additive so existing single-status doctest reports are unaffected (the doctest report Case model and its snapshots were not touched)

stages.name: mutation_checks
stages.reason: this task stabilizes the documentation-mutation surface itself; self-mutation dogfood of the mutation engine is gated to the Phase 7 stages (task 085). The stabilized contract is deterministic and covered by unit + snapshot + repeatability tests.

residual_risk: The stabilized mutation-aware report is exposed as a deterministic-core builder (mutation_experiment.stableMutationRun) plus the additive doctest.report.v1 schema extension; wiring it into the `zentinel doctest --mutate` CLI is a consumer step (cli.zig is outside this task's allowed scope) and the experimental zentinel.doctest.mutation_experiment.v1 report is intentionally left unchanged so its out-of-scope prototype test stays green.

The top-level doctest summary (preflight, non-mutation) vs summary.mutation split is documented in docs/DOCTEST_SPEC.md and represented in the schema; the stable builder emits the mutation slice (summary.mutation + mutation cases) that a full --mutate report composes with the preflight summary.
mutated_diff is a minimal deterministic original->replacement pair; the dm_/ds_ identities also include the unique mutant id, so identity uniqueness does not depend on diff granularity.
